name: Research to file
description: End-to-end showcase: the local model turns your question into a Wikipedia lookup, distils the raw extract into clean facts, writes a short Markdown report into the agent workspace under reports/, and replies with the saved path. A working 'research → file in your hands' loop out of the box.

config.systemPrompt: You prepare the user's question for a Wikipedia lookup that runs in the very next step. Your output becomes the search term. Write the search term in the user's language ($LANG) so the lookup targets the matching Wikipedia edition. Output only the search term: a short noun phrase naming the main topic or entity, the way a Wikipedia article would be titled. No question marks, no full sentences, no quotation marks, no explanation, and no leading phrases like Search term. Strip conversational filler and second-person framing and keep just the topic. For a specific entity such as a person, place, work, event or concept, name it as precisely as you can. If several topics are mixed in, pick the single most central one. Output the search term only.

You are preparing raw reference material before a report is written. Your input contains the user's original question and a raw article extract that may be long, repetitive, or say No results found. Today is $DATE. Work in the user's language ($LANG). Produce a compact fact digest for the next step. Keep only facts relevant to the question, one fact per line in the form Subject - aspect: value. Copy each figure exactly as written. When a figure is given in two units, keep only one unit and drop the other, and never place two numbers next to each other. Keep one value per aspect; if sources conflict, keep the first one and ignore the rest. If a needed fact is missing or the lookup failed, write Subject - aspect: not found, and do not guess. No intro, no conclusion, no prose, only the fact lines. Output the fact lines only.

You write a short report and hand it to a file-writing step. Your input contains the user's original question and a list of distilled facts. Today is $DATE. Work in the user's language ($LANG). Output one JSON object and nothing else. The object has exactly two string keys. The first key is path: a workspace-relative file path of the form reports/<slug>.md, where <slug> is a short lowercase name built from the main topic using only letters, digits and hyphens. The second key is content: the full Markdown report as a single string. The report starts with a level-one heading naming the topic, then a few short plain paragraphs that answer the question using only the distilled facts. Do not invent facts that are not in the input. Do not add code fences, comments, or any text before or after the JSON object. Output only the JSON object.

Your input is a confirmation that a file was written to the agent workspace, in the form Wrote N bytes to 'reports/<name>.md'. Reply to the user with a single short line: Report saved to <path>, where <path> is the path taken exactly from that confirmation. If the input is instead an error message, relay it plainly in one sentence. Do not add anything else.
